title: agent_cert.v1
description: The protocol-native Agent Certificate: the ship (issuer) binds an agent URI to its per-agent public key, as the payload of a Treeship receipt whose ENVELOPE signature by the ship key IS the certification. This is the intermediate link of the trust chain (card -> agent key -> ship root): a verifier who pins only the ship key can verify any of its agents' cards by walking through this artifact. Minted by `treeship agent register --own-key`; pushed by `treeship publish`; walked by `treeship resolve`. See docs/specs/registry-topology.md slice 1.

agent.description: Actor URI this certificate binds, e.g. agent://deployer.

subject_key_id.description: Key id of the agent's own signing key (the key this cert certifies).

subject_public_key.description: The agent key's public half, ed25519 base64url (no padding). A verifier uses THIS key to check the agent's card and receipts after verifying the cert envelope against a pinned Ship root.

issuer.description: Issuer URI, e.g. ship://<ship_id>. The envelope signer must be this ship's key.

valid_until.description: RFC 3339 expiry. Verifiers fail closed on expired certs.

model.description: Model the agent runs, when declared at registration.

description.description: Agent description, when declared at registration.
